title: Blind Source Separation (ICA / cocktail-party problem)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Comon 1994 ICA; Hyvarinen-Oja 2000 FastICA; Belouchrani 1997 SOBI; Zibulevsky-Pearlmutter 2001 sparse BSS; Smaragdis 1998 freq-domain ICA.

E.description: N independent sources s_k[t] are linearly mixed by an unknown channel matrix A in R^{M x N} producing M observed sensor signals y[t] = A*s[t]. The goal is to recover both A and s up to scale and permutation ambiguity using only statistical assumptions (non-Gaussianity, non-stationarity, sparsity, or temporal structure).
E.forward_model: y[t] = A * s[t] + n[t];  A unknown, s components statistically independent
E.world_state_x: Source signals S in R^{N x T}, columns of S statistically independent
E.observation_y: Sensor signals Y in R^{M x T}
physical_parameters_theta: Number of sources N, sensors M (typically M >= N for determined case)
Reverberation / convolutive channel order L_A (= 1 for instantaneous BSS)

W.regime: Identifiable when at most one source is Gaussian (Darmois-Skitovich theorem). Convolutive BSS requires additional constraint (temporal coloration or higher statistics). Underdetermined (N > M) requires sparsity prior.

C.solver_class: FastICA (deflation, symmetric), Infomax, JADE, SOBI (second-order blind), AMUSE, non-negative (NMF), deep (Conv-TasNet, DPRNN for audio)
C.error_bound: Inter-source interference SIR = |a_ii|^2 / sum_{j!=i}|a_ij|^2; CRLB-like bound for ICA: SIR -> 20*log10(sqrt(T/(N-1))) dB
C.complexity: FastICA: O(T*N^2) per iteration; JADE: O(N^4); SOBI: O(N*L^2) for L time lags
